summary: Iteration 2 testing for PMAISM follow-up changes. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests pass including new lead fields (qualification, passed_out_year, employment_status, previous_company, previous_role, years_experience) with both Fresher and Experienced demo payloads, GET /api/leads returns new fields, invalid email returns 422. Frontend: Verified consistent logo in navbar+footer, no custom cursor / no cursor-active class, Outcomes section DOM order above Success Stories, nav-link-outcomes scrolls to outcomes, hero-cta-demo opens lead-dialog with 3 sections (Personal/Education/Professional) and NO Apply toggle inside form, phone strips non-digits and caps at 10, invalid email + <10-digit phone blocked, Experienced reveals lead-prev-company/role/years-exp, Fresher hides them, valid Fresher submit shows lead-confirmation with 'Our Team will contact you within 1-2 Business Days' and Demo Session Details (Date/Time/Mode/Zoom Link 'We will message you the Zoom link before the session.'), footer-cta-demo opens lead-dialog.

minor.issue: Returns 200 OK on create; FastAPI convention prefers 201 Created (carried over from iter 1)

No server-side enforcement of 10-digit phone or qualification/passed_out_year/employment_status presence for lead_type='demo'; relies entirely on frontend validation. Public endpoint is also unrate-limited.

action_items: (Optional) Add server-side conditional validation: when lead_type='demo', require qualification, passed_out_year, employment_status; when employment_status='experienced', require previous_company/role/years_experience; enforce 10-digit phone via regex
Replace placeholder DEMO_SESSION.zoomLink and BRAND.whatsappLink before launch
critical_code_review_comments: LeadCreate model accepts arbitrary phone string — frontend regex /^\d{10}$/ enforces it, but API caller bypassing UI can submit any phone. Consider Pydantic constr(pattern=r'^\d{10}$').
lead_type Literal allows 'apply' though UI now always sends 'demo' — keep for backward compat but document.
QUALIFICATIONS list is frontend-only; backend accepts any string for qualification — no enum validation server-side.

Hero still has hero-cta-apply (Apply Now) calling openLead('apply'); per problem statement the form is Book Demo only and Apply buttons intentionally open the same demo form (LeadDialog ignores the type and always submits lead_type='demo'). This is correct per spec but the 'apply' param passed to openLead is unused inside LeadDialog — minor dead code.
server.py: logger still defined below routes (line ~33 looks fine actually, was earlier flagged but logger is at top now). Verified OK.
seed_data_creation: Created leads via POST /api/leads: TEST_Demo_User_V2 (experienced), TEST_Fresher_User (fresher), plus TEST_Apply_User and TEST_FE_User from form submission, all prefixed TEST_.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Lead form is single-flow Book Demo (no toggle). Data-testids: nav-logo, nav-link-outcomes, hero-cta-demo, footer-cta-demo, lead-dialog, lead-name, lead-email, lead-phone, lead-qualification (Radix Select - open trigger then click role=option), lead-passout-year, lead-status-fresher, lead-status-experienced, lead-prev-company, lead-prev-role, lead-years-exp, lead-submit, lead-confirmation, lead-confirmation-close. DOM order: Founder -> Placement(id=outcomes) -> SuccessStories(id=stories). DEMO_SESSION.zoomLink empty => 'We will message you the Zoom link before the session.'. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (8 tests).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Initial test-script assertion failure was due to icon prefixed whitespace in section labels (' Personal Details' vs 'Personal Details'); fixed by .trim() in the JS evaluation. No application code change required.
